rationale: The page contains detailed information about India's space missions, specifically the Gaganyaan Programme, the Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS), and the Aditya-L1 Mission. This content is highly relevant for generating questions about space technology and current scientific developments.
cleanedText:
Gaganyaan Programme
* About: It will be India's first Human Space Flight mission.
* Aim: Demonstration of human spaceflight capability by launching a crew of 3 members to an orbit of 400 km (LEO) for a 3-day mission and bring them back safely to earth.
* Gaganyatri will carry out shortlisted Indian microgravity research experiments onboard ISS.
* Components of the Gaganyaan
* Launch Vehicle Mark-3 (LVM-3): Formerly known as GSLV Mk-III, it is a 3-stage rocket.
* Orbital module: Consisting of Crew Module and Service Module.
* AI-powered Gynoid (female robot) Vyommitra (half-humanoid) will undertake the first uncrewed mission of Gaganyaan.
* The scope of the Programme has been extended to include building of first unit of the Bharatiya Antariksh Station.
* Successful launch of Gaganyaan will make India only the 4th country (after the US, Russia, and China) that have launched crewed spacecraft.
Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS)
* BAS is India's planned space station for scientific research.
* It will orbit around 400 – 450km above the Earth's surface. It will have five modules.
* Significance: Serves as a testing ground for studying how to keep astronauts safe and healthy on long-duration missions, facilitating microgravity research, etc.
4.3. ADITYA-L1 MISSION
Why in the News?
Using data from the Visible Emission Line Coronagraph (VELC) onboard Aditya-L1, scientists from the Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA), in collaboration with NASA, have successfully estimated key parameters of a Coronal Mass Ejection (CME).
About Aditya-L1 Mission
* India’s first dedicated solar mission.
* Objectives: To understand Coronal Heating and Solar Wind Acceleration; flares and near-earth space weather; Solar wind distribution and temperature anisotropy etc.
* Lifespan: 5 years
* 7 payloads:
* Remote Sensing Payloads: VELC, Solar Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope (SUIT), etc.
* In-situ Payloads: Aditya Solar wind Particle Experiment (ASPEX), etc.
* It placed in a halo orbit around the Lagrange point 1 (L1) of the Sun-Earth system, which is about 1.5 million km from the Earth.
* Halo orbit is a periodic, three-dimensional orbit at L1 involving Sun, Earth and a spacecraft.
* At Lagrange points, the gravitational pull of two large masses precisely equals the centripetal force required for a small object to move with them.
* These points allow continuous, uninterrupted observation of the Sun. Also, they help in reducing fuel consumption.
Other Major Solar Missions
* Hinotori (ASTRO-A) launched by Japan.
* Parker Solar Probe launched by US (first spacecraft to fly through the corona-the Sun’s upper atmosphere)
